title: Lecture Two Large-Gauge Holonomy Shift Context
summary: This equation context records why Witten's unit-flux background on S^2 times S^1 and the large gauge shift of the temporal holonomy are enough to force the Chern-Simons level to be integral.
assumptions: The background gauge field is compact.
The path integral only depends on the action modulo 2pi.
scope: Equation-level context for the large-gauge argument preceding the Hall-response theorem.

source_anchors.notes: Witten's S^2 times S^1 large-gauge background that makes the level quantization explicit.

representation: Equation context for the holonomy-shift argument that upgrades Chern-Simons gauge invariance into integrality of k.

math_expressions.notes: One unit of magnetic flux is inserted through the spatial two-sphere.
The Chern-Simons functional evaluates to the temporal holonomy angle.
A large gauge transformation shifts the holonomy by 2pi without changing the physical configuration.

math_expressions.latex: e^{ik\mathcal I_{\mathrm{CS}}} \text{ is gauge invariant } \Rightarrow k \in \mathbb Z
math_expressions.notes: Because the action is only defined modulo 2pi, the coefficient k must be integral.

symbols.meaning: temporal holonomy angle on the S^1 factor
Chern-Simons level multiplying the effective action

retrieval_hints: Open this before answering why k is an integer instead of merely asserting it.
